8.display: False — a square is always a rectangle, but not every rectangle is a square.

9.display: Sometimes — a square has four right angles but is not called a rectangle in some definitions; often stated as always a rectangle.

10.display: One pair of parallel lines (arrow marks) and one line perpendicular to them, labelled correctly.

11.display: No — a rhombus can have two pairs of equal sides without all angles being 90°.

12.display: Right-angle corner marked; perpendicular means meeting at 90°.

13.display: 6 sides and 6 vertices; unlike a square it has six sides, not four.

14.display: Trapezium (or trapezoid) — one pair of parallel sides.

15.display: Your own always/sometimes/never question with a correct answer.
